notes: FROZEN (agent=no): mistral-vibe is a PAYG API-key agent (Mistral / OpenAI-compatible provider) with no provider-managed subscription rate-limit usage window to burn down. RateLimitError (vibe/core/types.py:660) is a transient 429 throttle, not a persisted usage window; AgentStats (types.py:49) is cost/token accounting (session_cost + context_tokens), not a Windows[].UsedPercent; the only usage figure the vibe adapter reads is stats.context_tokens (sidecar.go), a context-window count reset to 0 on compaction (agent_loop.py:2008) — non-monotonic and context-scoped, violating the scenario's 'no reset to 0 / approaches cap without resetting'. The vibe parser emits no RateLimit snapshot (grep over the adapter finds none), unlike codex which carries rate_limits.primary.used_percent inside its tailed transcript. So the agent surfaces no rate-limit usage window → agent=no, daemon=n/a, driver=ready. Mirrors aider (PAYG, agent=no); distinct from kiro-cli/claudecode (agent=yes/incapable subscription products with out-of-band burndown). No lifecycle phases — not recordable. Unblock: a future vibe subscription tier emitting a rate-limit usage window into its transcript/meta.json.
